data.description:
## Symptom

When a tagged debug build (built via scripts/reload.sh --tag <name>) runs alongside the prod c11.app and then terminates, the prod c11's socket file at ~/Library/Application Support/c11/c11.sock can be unlinked from the filesystem as part of the debug build's shutdown cleanup. The prod c11 process remains alive and the FD bound to that path remains open inside the process, but no new CLI connection can reach the prod c11 because the bind point on disk is gone.

Net effect: every \`c11 <command>\` from any shell fails with "Socket not found at ~/Library/Application Support/c11/c11.sock" until the prod c11 is restarted. Restarting prod c11 hits C11-103 (state-dir migration is broken when both c11/ and c11mux/ exist), so the operator loses workspaces too. Two bugs compound.

## Observed 2026-05-18

Atin's session, ~21:21 local time. Two tagged debug builds were running alongside the prod c11.app:

- PID 40216  c11 DEV update-flow.app  etime 4h30m  built from /Users/atin/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/c11-update-flow/
- PID 58855  c11 DEV help-about.app   etime 6h30m  built from /Users/atin/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/c11-help-about/
- PID 29797  /Applications/c11.app    etime 4.5h   the prod release v0.48.0 (102), bundle id com.stage11.c11

Both debug builds terminated at ~21:21 (gone from \`ps -ax\` shortly after; cause of termination not yet determined). At the same timestamp:

- ~/Library/Application Support/c11/last-socket-path and /tmp/c11-last-socket-path both rewritten to "/var/folders/vr/.../T/csec-cmux-8EA984A6.sock" (and several other csec-cmux-* paths in a short burst around 21:17-21:21).
- The csec-cmux-* path itself never resolves to an actual bound socket on disk (find returns nothing).
- ~/Library/Application Support/c11/c11.sock (the stable default path the prod c11 had been bound to since 4:45pm) is unlinked from the filesystem.
- lsof -p 29797 still shows fd 4u bound to "/Users/atin/Library/Application Support/c11/c11.sock" — the kernel-level binding is intact, only the dentry is gone.
- /tmp/c11-last-cli-path still points at /Users/atin/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/c11-update-flow/Build/Products/Debug/c11 DEV update-flow.app/Contents/Resources/bin/c11 — strong indication that the update-flow build was the most-recent dev-shim writer.

The grep for "csec-cmux" returns zero matches across Sources/, Tests/, Resources/, vendor/, every worktree, and \`strings\` on the compiled prod c11 binary. The csec-cmux-* literal genuinely does not originate from any c11 build. Source of that string is currently unknown — possible candidates: a SwiftPM test fixture, a build-system temp-path generator, or upstream cmux code that has since been removed but lives in a Debug build's compiled artifacts. Worth chasing as part of the fix.

## Hypothesis: where the unlink happens

Sources/SocketControlSettings.swift::resolvedStableDefaultSocketPath uses this policy when picking a bind path:

\`\`\`swift
switch probeStableDefaultPathEntry(stableDefaultSocketPath) {
case .missing:                                            return stableDefaultSocketPath
case .socket(let owner) where owner == currentUserID:     return stableDefaultSocketPath
case .socket, .other, .inaccessible:                      return userScopedStableSocketPath(currentUserID)
}
\`\`\`

When a debug build starts, the stable default path is owned by the same user as the prod build (uid 501). The debug build's probe sees \`.socket(owner == currentUserID)\` and "uses" the stable default — but the actual socket-bind path the debug build picks is gated by \`shouldHonorSocketPathOverride\` (debug builds get override permission). So the debug build binds elsewhere (likely its own tagged path like /tmp/c11-debug-update-flow.sock) but the resolvedStableDefaultSocketPath logic above still thinks the stable default is "in use by us." On shutdown, the debug build runs socket cleanup that includes \`unlink(stableDefaultSocketPath)\` — assuming it's tidying its own file — and wipes the prod c11's bind point.

This is a hypothesis from reading; needs confirmation by tracing the shutdown path. Search terms: \`unlink\`, \`removeItem\`, \`stableDefaultSocketPath\` in SocketControlSettings and any cleanup hook in AppDelegate.

## Fix shape (sketch)

Pick one or compose:

1. Debug builds should never unlink the stable default socket path on shutdown. Only unlink your own bind path. If \`taggedDebugSocketPath()\` returned a different path than the stable default, ignore the stable default at cleanup time.
2. The unlink call should verify the file it's about to remove is the path THIS process bound. Stat + getsockname comparison would be sufficient.
3. Alternatively: prod c11 detects "my bind path was unlinked while I'm alive" and rebinds. Could be done via fsevents or a periodic stat. Higher complexity but more defensive.

(1) is probably cleanest — fixes the offending code path directly. (3) is a defense-in-depth follow-up.

## Severity

High. The compound failure mode is "developer runs a tagged debug build, then closes it, then the operator has lost CLI control over the prod c11 they use as their daily driver." Recovery requires restarting prod c11, which on a machine still affected by C11-103 also wipes the workspace state. Two stacked bugs, both observed in production on 2026-05-18.

## Test policy

Per code/c11/CLAUDE.md: tests verify observable behavior. Build a c11LogicTests test that simulates the dual-bind scenario via a fake FileManager / fake SocketControlSettings.probeStableDefaultPathEntry, exercises the debug-build's shutdown cleanup code path, and asserts the stable default path is left intact. No tests of source-code shape.

## References

- Sources/SocketControlSettings.swift — the bind-path resolution + override logic (around lines 487–540 in current main).
- C11-103 — state-dir migration bug, compounds with this one on operator workstations.
- Forensics conversation: 2026-05-18 C11-103 Orchestrator session, surface:7.
data.title: Tagged debug build shutdown unlinks prod c11's socket file, killing CLI control

data.body:
## Update 2026-05-18 (post-filing)

Two things to fold in:

### 1. Workaround confirmed

Cmd+Shift+P → command palette → "Restart CLI Listener" recovers the socket cleanly. Tested on Atin's machine 2026-05-18 ~21:54: socket file at `~/Library/Application Support/c11/c11.sock` was unlinked from the filesystem (prod c11.app PID 29797 still alive and bound at the path per `lsof`); running the menu action re-bound a fresh file at the same path, and `c11 identify` started working again. All surfaces, workspaces, panes, agent PTYs, and in-flight sub-agent dialogs preserved. The recovery code path is `AppDelegate.restartSocketListener(_:)` → `restartSocketListenerIfEnabled(source: "menu.command")` which calls `TerminalController.shared.stop()` + `start(...)` with a freshly-resolved socket path. Nothing touches `TabManager` or session state.

This workaround is now documented in `code/c11/CLAUDE.md` under "Pitfalls" so the next operator/agent who hits the symptom doesn't have to rediscover it.

### 2. Hypothesis NOT confirmed

The original ticket framed the debug-build-shutdown story as the suspect mechanism. After filing I went looking for the actual unlink code that would back it up, and **could not find one**.

- `grep -n "unlink\|removeItem\|removeFile" Sources/SocketControlSettings.swift` returns exactly ONE call (line 208) and it's `clearPassword()`, not socket teardown.
- `grep` in `AppDelegate.swift` for socket-cleanup-shaped terms near `terminate`/`stop`/`shutdown`/`teardown` returns nothing on the bind path.
- No broader matches for "remove sock", "unlink sock", "cleanupSocket", etc. across `Sources/`.

If shutdown were unlinking the stable default socket, the unlink call would be findable with those patterns. So the hypothesis as written is **not currently supported by code inspection**. The temporal correlation between debug-build shutdown (~21:21) and socket-file disappearance (~same window) is suspicious but not proof. A different mechanism could be at play: Sparkle updater, sandbox/TCC, an xcodebuild step from one of the in-flight C11-99 delegators working on `SocketControlSettings.swift`, or something I haven't enumerated.

### Recommendation

Demote the urgency on the "fix" portion of this ticket and split the work:

1. **C11-105 (this ticket) becomes a repro + diagnostic ticket.** Land an fseventsd watcher on `~/Library/Application Support/c11/c11.sock` that logs the offending PID when an unlink fires. Reproduce by running a tagged debug build alongside prod and tearing it down. If the hypothesis holds, the watcher names the process and the line; if it doesn't, the watcher names the actual culprit and a new fix-ticket gets filed against THAT.
2. The fix follow-up (whatever it ends up being) lands on a new ticket once the diagnostic surfaces ground truth.

The "Fix shape (sketch)" section in the original description should be treated as speculative until step 1 above lands. The "Workaround" section above is what operators and agents should reach for in the meantime.

PR open: https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11/pull/180 — fast-track delegator (single agent across plan / implement / review / PR). Diagnostic harness + runbook landed; fix ticket follows once the watcher names the culprit on a real reproduction.

Coverage:
- AC1-AC8 — AC7 deviates: tests live in the SwiftPM package via 'swift test' rather than c11LogicTests xctest. Boot prompt pre-approved either shape to avoid pbxproj churn.
- P1-P4 — manual demo of delete → rebound → delete cycle captured in PR body.

Note: boot prompt directed bumping to 'pr_open' as terminal state, but that's not in the lattice status enum (valid set: backlog, in_planning, planned, in_progress, review, done, blocked, needs_human, cancelled). Left at 'review' which matches PR-open semantics; orchestrator can bump to 'done' on merge.

PR #180 reframed and rebased onto a real fix (commit 186b3db77).

Root cause turned out to be the c11-logic test scheme itself — TerminalControllerSocketSecurityTests.swift was a c11LogicTests target member with a setUp() that called TerminalController.shared.stop(), and TerminalController.socketPath defaulted to SocketControlSettings.stableDefaultSocketPath. Every local `xcodebuild -scheme c11-logic test` invocation unlinked the prod c11's bind dentry. The tagged-debug-build hypothesis was wrong.

Three-part fix in this PR:
- Move the test back into c11Tests target (file-membership now matches its on-disk directory).
- TerminalController.socketPath defaults to "" with a non-empty guard in stop().
- SocketControlMode.cmuxOnly -> .c11Only with legacy "cmuxonly" parse compat preserved.

Regression tests live in c11Tests/SocketControlSafetyTests.swift (c11LogicTests target) — host-less, safe for local iteration. The kqueue watcher + runbook stay as a canary for any future unknown unlink source.

PR title updated. Ready for review.
Reconciliation: PR #180 (C11-105: fix socket unlink + keep kqueue watcher as canary) merged 2026-05-19. Work shipped and documented in CLAUDE.md Pitfalls. Board was stale at 'review'.
